sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Only 1 cell changed. Let me check what changed - it might have just changed one cell of the yellow object. Let me compare the before and after frames to understand what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what single cell changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Get ascii lines
before_lines = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_lines = after.ascii.split('\n')

# Find the changed cell
for r in range(64):
    for c in range(64):
        if before_lines[r][c] != after_lines[r][c]:
            print(f"Changed at row {r}, col {c}: '{before_lines[r][c]}' -> '{after_lines[r][c]}'")
            # Show context
            for dr in range(-2, 3):
                rr = r + dr
                if 0 <= rr < 64:
                    print(f"  {rr}: {after_lines[rr][max(0,c-3):c+4]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
